post.topic: handling claude api rate limits with exponential backoff
post.hook: How to handle Claude API rate limits like a pro (avoid 429 errors)
post.caption: Getting 429 errors from Claude API? Implement exponential backoff to handle rate limits gracefully. Start with a 1-second delay, then double it for each retry (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s). Add jitter by multiplying by a random factor between 0.5-1.5 to avoid thundering herd problems. Set a max retry limit (typically 3-5 attempts) and catch specific rate limit exceptions. This prevents your app from hammering the API and ensures better reliability. Pro tip: Monitor your retry patterns - if you're hitting limits frequently, consider upgrading your rate limits or implementing request queuing. Save this pattern for bulletproof API integration!
